text: Thich Nhat Hanh's concept of interbeing has a direct ethical implication: if the self is not separate from the world, then harming the world is self-harm. His Fourteen Mindfulness Trainings, developed for the Order of Interbeing in 1966 during the Vietnam War, translate this metaphysics into conduct — including the injunction not to avoid suffering but to remain present with it, both in oneself and in others. The trainings reframe ethics from rule-following to perceptual accuracy: violence arises from the illusion of separation. Right action, in this framework, follows naturally from clear seeing.
question: According to the passage, why did Thich Nhat Hanh develop the Fourteen Mindfulness Trainings?

options.text: To systematize Buddhist philosophy for Western academic audiences
To translate the metaphysics of interbeing into practical ethical conduct
To help monks cope with the psychological stress of monastic life
To replace traditional precepts with a more modern ethical framework
